name: Jucy Rentals
tagline: Budget-friendly, compact campervans — great value for families on a budget
best_for: Couples or families of 3 on a budget

pricing.per_day_note: Varies by season and van type. Summer peak: $150–189/day.

pros: Most affordable major campervan brand in NZ
cons: Older fleet — some vans showing wear
Not ideal for families of 4 in the 2-berth
verdict: Best value campervan hire in NZ. The 4-berth Cheeky works for small families; the Big Kahuna gives families of 4 breathing room.

name: Maui Campervans
tagline: Mid-range to premium campervans with a large, modern fleet
best_for: Families of 4 who want comfort and reliability
not_ideal_for: Budget travellers — Maui is 30–50% more expensive than Jucy
pros: Newer, more reliable fleet than budget operators
Better customer support and 24/7 roadside assistance
More spacious vans with better layouts
cons: Peak season availability books out fast
verdict: The best balance of quality and price for families. The Cascade 4-berth is the most popular family choice.

name: Britz Campervans
tagline: Family-focused campervans with separate sleeping zones
best_for: Families of 4–5 who prioritise space and separate sleeping areas

pricing.per_day_note: Summer peak: $250–320/day for the Explorer family van

pros: Best-designed family layouts — separate kids sleeping area in Explorer
Good size kitchen with full facilities
cons: Most expensive of the main brands
Peak season prices are very high
verdict: The #1 choice for families with young children who need separate sleeping spaces. The Explorer's layout genuinely works for families.

name: Mighty Campers
tagline: Value-for-money campervans — older fleet, great price
best_for: Budget-conscious families who prioritise cost over comfort
not_ideal_for: Those who want a premium experience

pricing.per_day_note: Often 10–20% cheaper than Jucy for equivalent vans

pros: Cheapest major campervan brand in NZ
cons: Oldest fleet — expect wear and dated interiors
verdict: Best price for families on a budget. Don't expect luxury — expect functional. Great if you're spending most of your time outside anyway.

name: Apollo Motorhomes
tagline: Premium motorhomes — best for families who want a home on wheels
best_for: Families who want maximum comfort on longer trips (14+ days)

pricing.per_day_note: The most expensive major operator. Premium justified for long trips.

pros: Newest fleet — vehicles typically less than 3 years old
Shower, toilet, and heating in all vans
cons: Large motorhomes harder to park in small towns
verdict: Worth it for 14+ day trips where the premium daily cost is amortised. For 7-day trips, consider Maui or Britz instead.
